title: dactylorhiza umbrosa var. afghanica
intro: Dactylorhiza umbrosa var. afghanica, also known as the Afghan marsh orchid, is a subspecies of the Dactylorhiza umbrosa orchid, a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the orchid family. The Afghan marsh orchid is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of the Afghan marsh orchid, including infusions and tinctures, can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties, but further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
